title: Application requirements and required documents
text: All applicants to Heinz College full-time master's programs submit an online application, official undergraduate transcripts, a professional resume, a required essay, and two letters of recommendation (three maximum). There is no application fee. International applicants may additionally need English-language proficiency exam results and a transcript evaluation. An optional video interview and additional essay are available. Essays must contain only true information and your own original content; AI-generated submissions risk automatic denial of admission. Heinz College does not have a required minimum GPA. Applications are submitted through the online portal at https://apply.heinz.cmu.edu/apply/.

title: Application deadlines
text: The Heinz College application opens September 1 each year for the following calendar year's January, May, and August intakes. Applicants still completing a bachelor's degree are encouraged to apply by the Round 2 deadline of January 10 so that fall-semester grades appear on their transcripts. Master's programs may continue to accept applications after the posted rounds if space remains. PhD programs do not accept applications after December 1. Applications for the August 2026 full-time cohort close June 15, 2026. Deadlines vary by program, so check the specific program page for exact dates.

title: Standardized testing (GRE/GMAT) requirements
text: The GRE or GMAT is required for admission to most Heinz College master's and PhD programs, though program-specific waivers are available. Requirements vary by program, so applicants should confirm on their specific program page. GRE scores may be up to five years old at the time of application. There is no required minimum GPA for admission.

title: English-language proficiency requirements
text: Applicants who are not native English speakers must submit English-language proficiency exam results (such as TOEFL, IELTS, or Duolingo). This requirement is waived for applicants who worked or studied for five or more years in an English-speaking country, or who completed four years of undergraduate education in the United States. TOEFL, IELTS, and Duolingo scores may be up to two years old at the time of application.

title: Tuition, scholarships, and financial aid
text: The Heinz College Office of Admissions gives merit-based scholarship consideration to every admitted student who indicates within their application that they wish to be considered; no separate financial aid application is required for scholarships. Scholarships are posted to student accounts before the start of the semester for registered students. Heinz College does not offer tuition waivers to master's students. Partner scholarships for Peace Corps, AmeriCorps, Teach For America, or City Year affiliates provide a minimum of $8,100 or $13,000 per semester. Federal loans and work-study are administered through The HUB (Carnegie Mellon's student services center) and require a FAFSA; work-study eligibility is limited to U.S. citizens and permanent residents. For official cost-of-attendance figures, consult the CMU tuition website.

title: Official admissions contacts
text: The Heinz College Office of Admissions can be reached by email at hnzadmit@andrew.cmu.edu or by phone at 412-268-2164. Program-specific admissions representatives are listed on the main admissions page. Prospective students with applicant-specific questions should contact the Office of Admissions directly.
